video_title: Top #DDD Videos in New York State with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: John's of 12
restaurants.owner_chef: Nicky Sid Nicky and Mikey Alpert
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in New York City. Love coming to this place. I mean, the old school tradition, all different kinds of food, especially some of my favorite, Italian. Now I'm in the East Village, and I hear that there's an Italian joint that's been open for 103 years. This is John's of 12. Starring a couple good fellas, Nicky Sid Nicky and Mikey Alpert.

dishes.description: Handmade veal meatballs, chopped veal, Italian bread soaked in milk, eggs, pecorino cheese, chopped scallions, caramelized onions, Worcestershire sauce, basil, dried oregano, salt, pepper, minced garlic, Marsala wine, Montaquia butter. Baked on a cookie sheet with marinara sauce, then prepared for service with olive oil, butter, Marsala wine for caramelization.
dishes.guy_response: I'm going to tell you something. That is one tender meatball. Incredibly flavorful. And this Marsala, that little bit of sweetness on the outside. Never have I had an all veal meatball. This is the way to do it. It's a beautiful thing. It is a beautiful thing. Thank you. Thank you. Nice job. 103 years old. Wouldn't be able to tell. There we go. Doesn't look a day over 90. Thank you. Thank you very much. It's just outrageous the way the meat is. It's unbelievable. They're so good. It's almost like a little sweet in your taste of veal.

restaurants.name: Pies and Thighs
restaurants.owner_chef: Sarah Sine, Carolyn Bain, and Erica Geldsailer
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, the greater New York area has one of the most eclectic mix of foods you could find anywhere. So when you're rolling out of Manhattan over the Williamsburg Bridge, you could expect to find some good old Southern food with a twist. This is Pies and Thighs. Sarah Sine, Carolyn Bain, and Erica Geldsailer.

dishes.description: Pork shoulder seasoned with a dry rub (paprika, cayenne, brown sugar, black pepper, kosher salt), smoked at 200 degrees for 9-10 hours, shredded, marinated in drippings, served on a bun with coleslaw and peppered vinegar (Carolina style).
dishes.guy_response: Not over smoked. Not overdone. Pork's super tender. Nice bark. Nice crust. Not pulled too thin. So you still get some really good texture. And that vinegar with the pepper at the end. Dynamite. Real deal. Thank you. I'm impressed. Pulled pork sandwich. The flavor's fantastic. I love how the bottom of the biscuit gets soggy by the end of the thing. You're kind of... You switch over to the fork and knife.

dishes.description: Chicken seasoned with paprika, cayenne, black pepper, brined for 24 hours in sugar and salt, dredged in all-purpose flour, then fried. Served with a stock wheat waffle, cinnamon butter, and blueberry compote.
dishes.guy_response: Not oily at all. Not too heavily battered. Good fried chicken. Thank you. When the chicken is this tender, you get all of that fry coming with it. That's crunchification right there. I'm gonna dig over here. I have to say that that is the most savory combination of chicken and waffles I've had. It's good fried chicken. Nice job. Thank you. Chicken and waffles. That crust is so sweet, and the chicken is so tender. On par, if not better, than what my grandmother used to make. The waffles, wow. Amazing.

restaurants.name: Eva's
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, one of my favorite parts of Triple D is the family joints. So I'm here in Syracuse, New York, right on the city line and about a mile and a half away from the fairgrounds, to check out a joint that's run by a Polish family that immigrated to the States in 88. This is Eva's. Eva Zachynski is the matchmaker, with some help from her daughter, Karolina, and son, David.

dishes.dish_name: Placki Gilew (Potato Pancake with Hungarian Sauce)
dishes.description: Potato pancake made from grated potatoes, onion, egg, pepper, seasoning, salt, flour. Served with Hungarian sauce made from ground beef, onion, peppers, garlic, diced tomato, tomato puree, paprika, cayenne, chili flake, pepper, salt, and spices. Topped with sour cream.
dishes.guy_response: That's really nice comfort food. Mmm. Potato pancake, nice and crisp outside, tender on the inside. And then this gravy, a little bit of spice to it. Oh, great. It's good. Thank you. Don't get down, Platsky, honey. I love to order these during the winter. I like when it starts to be fall time. Just all great. The fresh food, the presentation. Don't get any. That's it. It's the real deal. This is Easter. This is Christmas. This is everything rolled up into one.

restaurants.name: Grover's
restaurants.guy_intro: Here I am, East Amherst, New York, about 20 miles from downtown Buffalo, to visit a place that's been voted Best Burger of the Year. This place was actually the old hunting lodge for Grover Cleveland. Here we are at Grover's. That'd be Georgina Hartman, back when her mom, Joni, opened the place.

dishes.description: 12-ounce 80-20 certified Angus ground beef patty, cooked on a flat top with sauteed onions, butter, salt, pepper, Cajun spice (cayenne, paprika, black pepper, garlic), topped with roper crumbly blue cheese, served on a buttered bun.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. That's a big burger. This is the best part about this. Flat tops have a very distinct style about them, especially when you've got a good amount of fat. It creates this crunchy texture. I like Roquefort cheese. I like onions. But there's no way I could eat that whole thing. The people that are this big, those are the people that can eat two. Absolutely impossible. Is that enough burger? I mean, you guys are from Texas. Way too much for me. But it is excellent.

dishes.description: Soup made with butter, onions, water, chicken base, half and half, flour slurry, cheese sauce, ground burger, lettuce, and tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: No way. This is no kidding liquid cheeseburger. Super flavor. I mean, it's really awesome. Not too cheesy. Right size of tomato. And you know what makes it? The lettuce. It's got a little bit of sweetness to it. Glad you're enjoying it. This is like my 19th spoonful. That is so delicious. I love it, and it's wonderful. One cup of cheeseburger soup. Look at that monster bite. What do you got for me? Two thumbs up. You heard it right from him. And there's the cheeseburger soup for you.

dishes.description: Large chicken wings, fried at 400 degrees for 14-15 minutes until crunchy. Tossed in a sauce made with butter, fresh chopped garlic, grated parmesan cheese, and buffalo hot sauce.
dishes.guy_response: That's really good. Good deal. Super crunchilistic. Cooked just how I like it. Look at that little nugget right there. See all the fried? You get the sweetness of the butter and the parmesan cheese. A little bite of the garlic. I'd only kind of call it a hint of heat. But that's a great mixture. Boy, is that good. Your garlic parmesan wings. Really tender. Really crispy, which is really nice. Mmm. Good flavor. Nice garlic, good parmesan, nice meaty wing.

restaurants.name: Lake Effect Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am, Buffalo, New York. Well, about five miles northeast of Lake Erie at a joint that was once a diner and then changed into a Chinese restaurant, well, then transplanted from Pennsylvania and then turned back into a diner. These guys are curing their own ham, they're corning their own beef, and they're smoking their own bacon. This is Lake Effect Diner. That Tucker Curtin and his wife Erin brought back to life.

dishes.description: Boneless pork leg cured for 12-14 days in a brine (water, dried cloves, black peppercorns, dry mustard, light brown sugar, maple syrup, sodium nitrate, kosher salt), then slow-smoked for about three hours over mesquite charcoal, apple, and hickory woods, finished in the oven. Served with red-eye gravy made from smoked ham bones, pan juices, mirepoix (carrot, celery, onion), black peppercorn, kosher salt, Frank's hot sauce, fresh thyme, and coffee, simmered for three hours. Served with eggs and home fries.
dishes.guy_response: This is very flavorful. Tender ham, for sure. Like a side of ham with some ham. Being raised eating red-eye gravy, I've seen a lot of different variations. I do think that I would like to have a cup of red-eye gravy in a chow coffee. Awesome, bud. Good job. Ham, red-eye gravy. Very good. Very moist. Everything is really fresh. Really good. Really good.

dishes.description: Haddock seared on a flat top with garlic butter, topped with bruschetta (diced tomatoes, fresh basil, onion, chopped garlic, balsamic, salt, pepper) and white wine. Served with griddled mac and cheese and steamed broccoli. Finished with fresh lemon squeeze and parmesan cheese.
dishes.guy_response: That's got great texture. The fish is mega tender. You got the fresh tomato with the bruschetta mix. Griddled mac and cheese. I'm a fan of the fact that you guys make everything from scratch. But you keep it simple. Way to go, chef. All right. Italian fish, mac and cheese. It's really juicy. And the mac and cheese. On the flat top with the crunchy crunch? Yes. Come on now.

restaurants.name: The Pit Stop
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome to the pit stop. High-end food at a fast food price. All from high-end Italian chef Tony Rubato. Before bringing his own scratch-made menu to The Long Island.

dishes.description: Pork shoulder seared, then cooked in a stock pot with oil, chopped onions, celery, carrots, homemade chicken stock, and crushed tomatoes for 6-7 hours. Shredded pork is crisped on a flat-top griddle. Panini bread topped with homemade honey mustard, fire-roasted peppers, pickles, shredded pork, turkey, Virginia ham, and Swiss cheese, then pressed in a panini maker.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Pork's dynamite. The honey Dijon rocks. Love anything that's got red bell pepper in it. Good vinegar from the pickle. But the big calculation that makes this all tie together, the bread and the panini maker, that's really the kicker here. I mean, that is nice, crunchy. I mean, it's done right. Everything's melted in the center of it. I might recommend you have a good dental plan. Tasty sandwich.

restaurants.name: PastaBilities
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Armory Square in Syracuse, New York. This place here, I hear they make their own sauce, they make their own bread, and they make their own pasta. I mean, the pasta-bilities are endless. Thanks to owner Karen Kordeling, who's been perfecting the menu since 82.

dishes.description: Duck breast cured for seven days with a rub (minced garlic, cracked pepper, sugar, fresh rosemary, fresh sage, salt, pink salt), then smoked for 1.5 hours over lump charcoal and apple wood. A pomegranate syrup is made. Flatbread topped with garlic oil, fresh mozzarella, dried cherries, caramelized onions, Humboldt Fog goat cheese, and sliced smoked duck bacon. Baked for 12 minutes, then finished in a salamander for crispness, topped with more pomegranate syrup, cracked pepper, basil, and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: The crust on this flatbread is righteous. So the bread has such great hue. The fresh mozz, tartness of that cherry, a little bit of funkaliciousness coming from that Humboldt Fog. Nobody takes the lead role except the duck bacon. It's probably. One of the best duck dishes I've ever had. Yeah, you jackpotted on that one, girl. Wow. Excellent. Flatbread pizza's up. It's probably one of the best pizzas I've ever had. This is baller. It's awesome. Baller's my word.

dishes.description: Rigatoni pasta with a spicy tomato cream sauce. Sauce made with olive oil, garlic, sweet onion, yellow peppers, sweet red peppers, green peppers, jalapeno, oregano, red chili flake, cayenne, salt, dried basil, black pepper, house tomato sauce, 'wicked' chili spice oil (chipotle peppers, chili peppers), cream, sugar, and Romano cheese. Sliced chicken breast seasoned with salt and pepper, browned, then combined with the sauce and rigatoni. Garnished with Locatelli cheese and green onion.
dishes.guy_response: Winner, winner. Wicked chicken dinner. You know, not only have you beat me in the blonde department, the height department, but now you just rolled out winner, winner, wicked chicken dinner. That's an awesome dish. I'm so glad. Pasta's nice. Not too much sauce. Not overly spicy. A real simple dish, but quality ingredients made the right way. And it's a staple here at PastaBilities. That's right. Love it.
